name: Zerodha Trading Platform — Full Kafka Producer + Consumer Pipeline
scenario: Complete the Zerodha Kafka event pipeline: order-service publishes trades, notification-service and portfolio-service consume them. This is the async backbone of the trading platform.
description: Three services: order-service (producer), notification-service (consumer 1), portfolio-service (consumer 2). Both consumers on 'trade-executed'. Different groups. Retry + DLT on notification. Concurrency=3 on portfolio. End-to-end @EmbeddedKafka integration test.

core.description: `TradeNotificationConsumer` @Service: @KafkaListener(topics='trade-executed', groupId='notification-group') + @RetryableTopic(attempts=3, backoff=500ms×2) + @DltHandler that logs to DLT database.
`PortfolioUpdateConsumer` @Service: @KafkaListener(topics='trade-executed', groupId='portfolio-group', concurrency='3'). Updates holdings: BUY adds, SELL subtracts qty for symbol.
Consumer application.properties (notification-service): JsonDeserializer, trusted.packages=com.codequest.events, auto-offset-reset=earliest, group-id=notification-group.
TradeEventPublisher (from T16.2) still works: publishes TradeExecutedEvent with symbol as key to 'trade-executed'.

@EmbeddedKafka @SpringBootTest integration tests (4): (1) notification consumer receives trade + SMS sent, (2) portfolio consumer receives trade + holding updated, (3) both consumers receive same message (separate groups), (4) DLT receives message when processing always fails. All 4 pass.

bonus.description: Add batch consumer for analytics: @KafkaListener(topics='trade-executed', groupId='analytics-batch', batch='true'). Inserts List<TradeExecutedEvent> into analytics DB in one batch call. @EmbeddedKafka test: publish 5 trades, verify batch of 5 received.

Add manual acknowledgment: configure ack-mode=manual on portfolio consumer. Portfolio.updateHolding() is idempotent. Only acknowledge if holding update succeeds. Test: simulate failure, verify message redelivered.

Add a ConsumerRecord version of the audit consumer (groupId='audit-group') that logs topic, partition, offset, key for every trade. @EmbeddedKafka test verifies correct partition/key in ConsumerRecord.

data_to_use:
Test trades:
1. BUY 10 TCS @ ₹3890 by U01
2. SELL 5 RELIANCE @ ₹2450 by U02
3. BUY 20 INFY @ ₹1560 by U01

Expected notification SMS:
- 'Trade: 10 TCS @ ₹3890'
- 'Trade: 5 RELIANCE @ ₹2450'

Expected portfolio updates:
- U01 TCS: +10 shares
- U02 RELIANCE: -5 shares

@EmbeddedKafka topics: trade-executed, trade-executed-retry-0, trade-executed-retry-1, trade-executed-dlt
example_output:
# order-service publishes:
[TradeEventPublisher] Trade event published: TRD-TCS-001 partition=0 offset=42

# notification-service receives:
[notification-group] TradeNotificationConsumer received: TCS x10 @ ₹3890 for U01
[SmsService] SMS sent to U01: Trade: 10 TCS @ ₹3890

# portfolio-service receives (same message, different group):
[portfolio-group] PortfolioUpdateConsumer received: TCS x10 BUY for U01
[PortfolioService] Updated U01 TCS: +10 → total 10 shares

# Tests:
✓ notification_smsSent
✓ portfolio_holdingUpdated
✓ bothGroupsReceiveSameMessage
✓ dltCalledAfterRetryExhaustion
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. trusted.packages MUST be configured for JsonDeserializer — the #1 forgotten consumer property. @EmbeddedKafka topics list must include ALL topics created by @RetryableTopic (topic-retry-0, topic-retry-1, topic-dlt). Different groupId = independent consumption of same messages. CountDownLatch preferred over Thread.sleep for test synchronization.
submission_instructions: GitHub push. All 4 @EmbeddedKafka tests pass. README explains: same topic, two consumer groups, why they both receive every message.
tips_for_success: trusted.packages: mandatory for JsonDeserializer; use * in tests for simplicity
@EmbeddedKafka topics: include retry and DLT topics when using @RetryableTopic
CountDownLatch in consumer: latch.countDown() when processed; test: latch.await(5,SECONDS)
Different groupId: both consumers INDEPENDENTLY receive every 'trade-executed' message
@DltHandler receives the event directly (not wrapped in an exception)
concepts_reinforced: @KafkaListener with groupId for independent consumer groups
CountDownLatch for async consumer test synchronization
